settings.playbackSpeedDescription: Adjust the speed of voice playback.

settings.revokeTokenWarning: will lose access to Onyx. This action cannot be undone.

agents.title: Agents

agents.noAgentsMatch: No agents match the current search.
agents.failedToLoadAgents: Failed to load agents. Please try refreshing the page.
agents.deleteAgentConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete {name}? This action cannot be undone.
agents.removeFeaturedDescription: This will remove {name} from the featured section on top of the explore agents list. New users will no longer see it pinned to their sidebar, but existing pins are unaffected.
agents.featureDescription: Featured agents appear at the top of the explore agents list and are automatically pinned to the sidebar for new users with access. Use this to highlight recommended agents across your organization.
agents.doesNotChangeAccess: This does not change who can access this agent.
agents.unlistDescription: Unlisted agents don't appear in the explore agents list but remain accessible via direct link, and to users who have previously used or pinned them.

users.title: Users & Requests
users.name: Name
users.noUsersMatch: No users match the current filters.
users.failedToLoadUsers: Failed to load users. Please try refreshing the page.
users.upcomingChangesDesc: Onyx is transitioning to group-based permissions for more granular access control. Curator and Global Curator roles will be replaced by configurable group permissions. We recommend reviewing current role assignments to ensure a smooth transition.

slackBots.title: Slack Integration
slackBots.description: Setup Slack bots that connect to Onyx. Once setup, you will be able to ask questions to Onyx directly from Slack. Additionally, you can:
slackBots.feature1: Setup OnyxBot to automatically answer questions in certain channels.
slackBots.feature2: Choose which document sets OnyxBot should answer from, depending on the channel the question is being asked.
slackBots.feature3: Directly message OnyxBot to search just as you would in the web UI.
slackBots.followGuide: found in the Onyx documentation to get started!
slackBots.noBots: Please add a New Slack Bot to begin chatting with Danswer!
slackBots.referGuide: if you are not sure how to get these tokens!
slackBots.userTokenSubtext: Optional: User OAuth token for enhanced private channel access
slackBots.deleteConfirmMessage: Are you sure you want to delete this Slack bot? This action cannot be undone.
slackBots.failedToDeleteConfig: Failed to delete Slack bot config
slackBots.noChannelConfigs: No channel-specific configurations. Add a new configuration to customize behavior for specific channels.
slackBots.defaultConfigDescription: This default configuration will apply to all channels and direct messages (DMs) in your Slack workspace.
slackBots.disableDefaultWarning: Warning: Disabling the default configuration means OnyxBot won't respond in Slack channels unless they are explicitly configured. Additionally, OnyxBot will not respond to DMs.
slackBots.channelNameSubtext: Enter the name of the Slack channel (without the # symbol)
slackBots.allPublicSublabel: Let OnyxBot respond based on information from all public connectors
slackBots.specificDocSetsSublabel: Control which documents to use for answering questions
slackBots.searchAgentSublabel: Control both the documents and the prompt to use for answering questions
slackBots.nonSearchAgentSublabel: Chat with an agent that does not use documents
slackBots.selectDocSetsForSlack: Select the document sets OnyxBot will use while answering questions in Slack.
slackBots.unselectableDocSets: These document sets cannot be attached as they have auto-synced docs:
slackBots.disabledTooltip: Unable to use this document set because it contains a connector with auto-sync permissions. OnyxBot's responses in this channel are visible to all Slack users, so mirroring the asker's permissions could inadvertently expose private information.
slackBots.selectSearchAgentForSlack: Select the search-enabled agent OnyxBot will use while answering questions in Slack.
slackBots.syncAgentNote: Note: Some of your agents have auto-synced connectors in their document sets. You cannot select these agents as they will not be able to answer questions in Slack.
slackBots.selectNonSearchAgentForSlack: Select the non-search agent OnyxBot will use while answering questions in Slack.
slackBots.answerTypeTooltip: Controls the format of OnyxBot's responses.
slackBots.onlyRespondIfCitationsTooltip: If set, will only answer questions where the model successfully produces citations
slackBots.showContinueInWebUi: Show Continue in Web UI button
slackBots.showContinueTooltip: If set, will show a button at the bottom of the response that allows the user to continue the conversation in the Onyx Web UI
slackBots.stillNeedHelpTooltip: OnyxBot's response will include a button at the bottom of the response that asks the user if they still need help.
slackBots.usersGroupsTagSubtext: The Slack users / groups we should tag if the user clicks the "Still need help?" button. If no emails are provided, we will not tag anyone and will just react with a 🆘 emoji to the original message.
slackBots.usersGroupsTagPlaceholder: User email or user group name...
slackBots.onlyRespondToQuestionsTooltip: If set, OnyxBot will only respond to messages that contain a question mark
slackBots.respondToOnyxBotOnlyTooltip: If set, OnyxBot will only respond when directly tagged
slackBots.respondToBotMessagesTooltip: If not set, OnyxBot will always ignore messages from Bots
slackBots.respondEphemeral: Respond to user in a private (ephemeral) message
slackBots.respondEphemeralTooltip: If set, OnyxBot will respond only to the user in a private (ephemeral) message. If you also chose 'Search' Agent above, selecting this option will make documents that are private to the user available for their queries.
slackBots.respondToCertainUsersSubtext: If specified, OnyxBot responses will only be visible to the members or groups in this list.
slackBots.privacyAlertText: Please note that if the private (ephemeral) response is *not selected*, only public documents within the selected document sets will be accessible for user queries. If the private (ephemeral) response *is selected*, user quries can also leverage documents that the user has already been granted access to. Note that users will be able to share the response with others in the channel, so please ensure that this is aligned with your company sharing policies.
slackBots.removedDocSetsWarning: We removed one or more document sets from your selection because they are no longer valid. Please review and update your configuration.

connectors.deletionErrorTooltip: This error occurred while attempting to delete the connector. You may re-attempt a deletion by clicking the "Delete" button.
connectors.resolvingAllErrors: Currently attempting to resolve all errors by performing a full re-index. This may take some time to complete.
connectors.indexingErrorsDescription: Below are the errors encountered during indexing. Each row represents a failed document or entity.
connectors.indexingErrorsResolveDescription: Click the button below to kick off a full re-index to try and resolve these errors. This full re-index may take much longer than a normal update.
connectors.noErrorsOnPage: No errors found on this page
connectors.noIndexingAttemptsDescription: Index attempts are scheduled in the background, and may take some time to appear. Try refreshing the page in ~30 seconds!
connectors.totalDocsTooltip: Total number of documents replaced in the index during this indexing attempt
connectors.alsoRemovedDocs: (also removed {count} docs that were detected as deleted in the source)
connectors.reindexTooltip: This index attempt {is} a full re-index. All documents from the source {being} synced into the system.
connectors.cannotRemoveAllFiles: Cannot remove all files from a connector. Delete the connector if this is desired.
connectors.filesUpdatedSuccess: Files updated successfully! Document index is being updated in the background. New files are being indexed and removed files will be pruned from the search results.
connectors.confirmFileChangesDescription: When you save these changes, the following will happen:
connectors.documentsPrunedFromIndex: Documents from these files will be pruned from the Document Index
connectors.newFilesIndexed: New files will be uploaded, chunked, embedded, and indexed in the Document Index
connectors.runIndexingDescription: This will pull in and index all documents that have changed and/or have been added since the last successful indexing run.
connectors.completeReindexDescription: This will cause a complete re-indexing of all documents from the source.
connectors.noteDependingOnDocs: NOTE: depending on the number of documents stored in the source, this may take a long time.
connectors.unexpectedErrorIndexing: An unexpected error occurred while trying to start indexing
connectors.cannotReindexWhileIndexing: Cannot re-index while indexing is already in progress
connectors.fixConfigBeforeReindexing: Fix the connector configuration before re-indexing
connectors.invalidConnectorDescription: This connector is in an invalid state. Please update your credentials or create a new connector before re-indexing.
connectors.ranIntoIssues: We ran into some issues while processing some documents.
connectors.refreshFreqDetails: How often the connector should refresh (in minutes)
connectors.pruningFreqDetails: How often the connector should be pruned (in hours)
connectors.invalidRefreshFrequency: Invalid refresh frequency: must be an integer
connectors.failedToUpdateRefreshFreq: Failed to update connector refresh frequency
connectors.invalidPruningFrequency: Invalid pruning frequency: must be a valid number
connectors.failedToUpdatePruningFreq: Failed to update connector pruning frequency
connectors.failedToFetchConnectorInfo: Failed to fetch info on Connector with ID {id}
connectors.deletingConnectorDescription: Deleting this connector schedules a deletion job that removes its indexed documents and deletes it for every user.
connectors.failedToScheduleDeletion: Failed to schedule deletion of connector - {error}
connectors.clickToEditConnector: Click to edit the existing connector.
connectors.clickToManageSlack: Click to manage your Slack connector.
connectors.notValidConnector: '{connector}' is not a valid Connector Type!
connectors.oauthFailed: OAuth authentication failed. Please try again.
connectors.operationTimedOut: Operation timed out after {seconds} seconds. Check your configuration for errors?
connectors.federatedOptionTooltip: A federated search option is available for this connector. It will result in greater latency and reduced search quality.
connectors.invalidConnectorConfirmDetails: This connector was previously marked as invalid. Please verify that your configuration is correct before re-enabling. Are you sure you want to proceed?
connectors.retrievingConnectorDetails: Retrieving connector details and credential schema
connectors.connectorNameDescription: A descriptive name for the connector.
connectors.pruneFrequencyDescription: Checks all documents against the source to delete those that no longer exist. Note: This process checks every document, so be cautious when increasing frequency. Default is 720 hours (30 days). Decimal hours are supported (e.g., 0.1 hours = 6 minutes). Enter 0 to disable pruning for this connector.
connectors.refreshFrequencyDescription: This is how frequently we pull new documents from the source (in minutes). If you input 0, we will never pull new documents for this connector.
connectors.indexingStartDateDescription: Documents prior to this date will not be pulled in

google.failedToLoadGdCredentials: Failed to load Google Drive credentials.
google.errorLoadingAppCredentials: Error loading app credentials. Contact an administrator.
google.selectOrDragJson: Select or drag JSON credentials file...
google.deleteConnectorsFirst: Please delete all associated connectors, then try again.
google.adminEmailHint: Enter the email of an admin/owner of the Google Organization that owns the account(s) you want to index.

google.toConnectGoogleDrive: To connect your Google Drive, create credentials (either OAuth App or Service Account), download the JSON file, and upload it below.
google.successfullyRevokedGoogleDriveCredential: Successfully revoked the Google Drive credential!
google.cannotRevokeWhileConnectorAssociated: Cannot revoke the Google Drive credential while any connector is still associated with the credential. Please delete all associated connectors, then try again.
google.credentialsSuccessfullyUploadedAuthentic: Your credentials have been successfully uploaded and authenticated.
google.completeStep1First: Please complete Step 1 by uploading either OAuth credentials or a Service Account key before proceeding with authentication.

google.failedToCreateServiceAccountCredential: Failed to create service account credential - {error}
google.nextAuthenticateGoogleDriveOAuth: Next, you need to authenticate with Google Drive via OAuth. This gives us read access to the documents you have access to in your Google Drive account.

google.nextAuthenticateGmailOAuth: Next, you need to authenticate with Gmail via OAuth. This gives us read access to the emails you have access to in your Gmail account.

configuration.unstructuredDescription: Unstructured extracts and transforms complex data from formats like .pdf, .docx, .png, .pptx, etc. into clean text for Onyx to ingest. Provide an API key to enable Unstructured document processing.
configuration.unstructuredNote: Note: this will send documents to Unstructured servers for processing.
configuration.deleteApiKeyBeforeUpdate: Delete the current API key before updating.
configuration.failedToFetchEmbeddingStatus: Failed to fetch embedding model status
configuration.searchSettingsOutdated: Your search settings are currently out of date! We recommend updating your search settings and re-indexing.
configuration.cancelSwitchConfirm: Are you sure you want to cancel? Cancelling will revert to the previous model and all progress will be lost.
configuration.currentlySwitchingTo: Currently in the process of switching to: {model}
configuration.switchingModelsInstantDesc: You're currently switching embedding models, and you've selected the instant switch option. The transition will complete shortly.
configuration.newModelActiveSoon: The new model will be active soon.
configuration.switchingModelsNoConnectors: You're currently switching embedding models, but there are no connectors to reindex. This means the transition will be quick and seamless!
configuration.allConnectorsPausedSwitchover: All connectors are currently paused, so none are blocking the switchover. Paused connectors will keep re-indexing in the background.

kg.kgDescription: The Knowledge Graph feature lets you explore your data in new ways. Instead of searching through unstructured text, your data is organized as entities and their relationships, enabling powerful queries like:
kg.queryExample1: - "Summarize my last 3 calls with account XYZ"
kg.queryExample2: - "How many open Jiras are assigned to John Smith, ranked by priority"
kg.kgSetupNote: (To use Knowledge Graph queries, you'll need a dedicated Assistant configured in a specific way. Please contact the Onyx team for setup instructions.)
kg.gettingStartedDescription: Begin by configuring some high-level attributes, and then define the entities you want to model afterwards.
kg.vendorDomainsDescription: Domain names of your company. Users with these email domains will be recognized as employees.
kg.ignoreDomainsDescription: Domain names to ignore. Users with these email domains will be excluded from the Knowledge Graph.
kg.coverageStartDescription: The start date of coverage for Knowledge Graph.
kg.connectSomeConnectors: To configure Knowledge Graph, first connect some

discordBots.title: Discord Integration
discordBots.description: Connect Onyx to your Discord servers. Users can ask questions directly in Discord channels.
discordBots.configuredMessage: Your Discord bot token is configured.
discordBots.toChangeToken: To change the token, delete the current one and add a new one.
discordBots.enterToken: Enter your Discord bot token to enable the bot. You can get this from the Discord Developer Portal.
discordBots.deleteConfirmDetails: This will disconnect your Discord bot. You will need to re-enter the token to use the bot again.
discordBots.registrationKeyDescription: This key will only be shown once!
discordBots.copyCommand: Copy the command and send it from any text channel in your server!
discordBots.serverMustRegister: Server must be registered before it can be enabled
discordBots.noServersDescription: Create a server configuration to get started.
discordBots.deleteServerConfirm: This will remove all settings for this Discord server.
discordBots.channelConfigDescription: Run !sync-channels in Discord to update the channel list.
discordBots.waitingForRegistrationDesc: Use the !register command in your Discord server with the registration key to complete setup.
discordBots.channelConfigUnavailable: Channel configuration will be available after the server is registered.
discordBots.defaultAgentDescription: The agent used by the bot in all channels unless overridden.
discordBots.noChannelsDescription: Run !sync-channels in Discord to add channels.

actions.mcpDescription: Connect MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers to add custom actions and tools for your agents.
actions.openApiDescription: Connect OpenAPI servers to add custom actions and tools for your agents.

serviceAccounts.description: Use service accounts to programmatically access Onyx API.
serviceAccounts.checkConsoleDetails: Please check the console for more details.
serviceAccounts.upgradePlan: Upgrade to a paid plan to create API keys.
serviceAccounts.upgradePlanDescription: Trial accounts do not include API key access — purchase a paid subscription to unlock this feature.
serviceAccounts.emptyStateText: Create service account API keys with user-level access.
serviceAccounts.adminRoleDescription: Unrestricted admin access to all endpoints.
serviceAccounts.basicRoleDescription: Standard user-level access to non-admin endpoints.
serviceAccounts.limitedRoleDescription: For agents: chat posting and read-only access to other endpoints.
serviceAccounts.apiKeyModalDescription: Save this key before continuing. It won't be shown again.
serviceAccounts.regenerateDescription: Your current API key *{name}* (`{display}`) will be revoked and a new key will be generated. You will need to update any applications using this key with the new one.
serviceAccounts.deleteAccountDescription: Any application using the API key of account *{name}* (`{display}`) will lose access to Onyx.
serviceAccounts.createDescription: Use service account API key to programmatically access Onyx API with user-level permissions. You can modify the account details later.

debug.description: **Debug Logs** provide detailed information about system operations and events. You can download logs for each category to analyze system behavior or troubleshoot issues.

scim.title: SCIM
scim.description: Sync users and groups via System for Cross-domain Identity Management (SCIM) protocol.
scim.failedToLoadToken: Failed to load SCIM token status.
scim.somethingWentWrong: Something went wrong. Please try again.
scim.regenerateWarning: Your current SCIM token will be revoked and a new token will be generated. You will need to update the token on your identity provider before SCIM provisioning will resume.
scim.tokenDescription: Save this key before continuing. It won't be shown again.
scim.syncDescription: Connect your identity provider to import and sync users and groups.
scim.provideKeyHint: Provide the SCIM key to your identity provider to begin syncing users and groups.

migration.description: Monitor the migration from Vespa to OpenSearch and control the active retrieval source.
migration.retrievalDescription: Controls which document index is used for retrieval.

rateLimits.description: Token rate limits enable you control how many tokens can be spent in a given time period. With token rate limits, you can:
rateLimits.setGlobalLimit: Set a global rate limit to control your team's overall token spend.
rateLimits.setUserLimit: Set rate limits for users to ensure that no single user can spend too many tokens.
rateLimits.setGroupLimit: Set rate limits for user groups to control token spend for your teams.
rateLimits.enableDisable: Enable and disable rate limits on the fly.
rateLimits.globalDescription: Global rate limits apply to all users, user groups, and API keys. When the global rate limit is reached, no more tokens can be spent.
rateLimits.userDescription: User rate limits apply to individual users. When a user reaches a limit, they will be temporarily blocked from spending tokens.
rateLimits.userGroupDescription: User group rate limits apply to all users in a group. When a group reaches a limit, all users in the group will be temporarily blocked from spending tokens, regardless of their individual limits. If a user is in multiple groups, the most lenient limit will apply.
rateLimits.failedToLoad: Failed to load token rate limits
rateLimits.validationTimeRequired: Time Window is a required field
rateLimits.validationTimeMin: Time Window must be at least 1 hour
rateLimits.validationBudgetRequired: Token Budget is a required field
rateLimits.validationBudgetMin: Token Budget must be at least 1
rateLimits.validationScopeRequired: Target Scope is a required field
rateLimits.validationGroupRequired: User Group is a required field

groups.title: Groups
groups.upcomingChangesDesc: Onyx is transitioning to group-based permissions, enabling more flexible access control through configurable permissions per group. We recommend reviewing your group structure to prepare for this update.
groups.createGroupsDescription: Create groups to organize users and manage access.
groups.checkConsoleDetails: Please check the console for more details.
groups.basicGroupDescription: Default group for all users with basic permissions.
groups.adminGroupDescription: Built-in admin group with full access to manage all permissions.
groups.noPrivateResources: No private connectors / document sets / agents
groups.groupNotFoundDescription: This group doesn't exist or may have been deleted.
groups.name: Name
groups.groupSyncing: This group is currently syncing. Please wait a moment and try again.
groups.syncingTooltip: Document embeddings are being updated due to recent changes to this group.
groups.deleteGroupDescription: Members will lose access to any resources shared with this group.
groups.deleteGroupConfirmMessage: Members of group {name} will lose access to any resources shared with this group, unless they have been granted access directly. Deletion cannot be undone.
groups.tokenRateLimitDescription: Limit number of tokens this group can use within a given time period.
groups.sharedWithThisGroupDescription: Share connectors, document sets, agents with members of this group.
groups.noConnectorsOrDocSets: No connectors or document sets added
groups.noConnectorsOrDocSetsDescription: Add connectors or document set to share with this group.
groups.noAgentsAddedDescription: Add agents to share with this group.

llm.connectAnotherProvider: Connect another provider to continue using chats.

chatPreferences.description: Organization-wide chat settings and defaults. Users can override some of these in their personal settings.
chatPreferences.teamNameDescription: This is added to all chat sessions as additional context to provide a richer/customized experience.
chatPreferences.teamContextDescription: Users can also provide additional individual context in their personal settings.
chatPreferences.describeTeam: Describe your team and how Onyx should behave.
chatPreferences.systemPromptDescription: Base prompt for all chats, agents, and projects. Modify with caution: Significant changes may degrade response quality.
chatPreferences.systemPromptModalDescription: This base prompt is prepended to all chats, agents, and projects.
chatPreferences.systemPromptWarningDescription: System prompt affects all chats, agents, and projects. Significant changes may degrade response quality.
chatPreferences.searchModeDescription: UI mode for quick document search across your organization.
chatPreferences.setupConnectorsForSearch: Set up connectors to use Search Mode
chatPreferences.deepResearchDescription: Agentic research system that works across the web and connected sources. Uses significantly more tokens per query.
chatPreferences.chatAutoScrollDescription: Automatically scroll to new content as chat generates response. Users can override this in their personal settings.
chatPreferences.actionsAndToolsDescription: Tools and capabilities available for chat to use. This does not apply to agents.
chatPreferences.internalSearchDescription: Search through your organization's connected knowledge base and documents.
chatPreferences.imageGenerationDescription: Generate and manipulate images using AI-powered tools.
chatPreferences.imageGenerationTooltip: Image generation requires a configured model. Set one up under Configuration > Image Generation, or ask an admin.
chatPreferences.webSearchDescription: Search the web for real-time information and up-to-date results.
chatPreferences.openUrlDescription: Fetch and read content from web URLs.
chatPreferences.authenticateMcp: Authenticate this MCP server before enabling its tools.
chatPreferences.keepChatHistoryDescription: Specify how long Onyx should retain chats in your organization.
chatPreferences.fileSizeLimitDescription: Files attached in chats and projects must fit within both limits to be accepted. Larger files increase latency, memory usage, and token costs.
chatPreferences.allowAnonymousDescription: Allow anyone to start chats without logging in. They do not see any other chats and cannot create agents or update settings.
chatPreferences.alwaysStartWithAgentDescription: This removes the default chat. Users will always start in an agent, and new chats will be created in their last active agent. Set featured agents to help new users get started.

voice.pageDescription: Configure speech-to-text and text-to-speech providers for voice input and spoken responses.
voice.speechToTextDescription: Select a model to transcribe speech to text in chats.
voice.textToSpeechDescription: Select a model to speak out chat responses.
voice.connectSttProvider: Connect a speech to text provider to use in chat.
voice.connectTtsProvider: Connect a text to speech provider to use in chat.
voice.whisperDescription: OpenAI's general purpose speech recognition model.
voice.azureSpeechSttDescription: Speech to text in Microsoft Foundry Tools.
voice.azureSpeechTtsDescription: Text to speech in Microsoft Foundry Tools.
voice.tts1Description: OpenAI's text-to-speech model optimized for speed.
voice.tts1HdDescription: OpenAI's text-to-speech model optimized for quality.
voice.disconnectWarning: **{name}** models will no longer be used for speech-to-text or text-to-speech, and it will no longer be your default. Session history will be preserved.
voice.connectAnotherProvider: Connect another provider to continue using voice.
voice.disconnectNonDefaultWarning: **{name}** models will no longer be available for voice.

chat.inputPlaceholder: How can I help you today?
chat.voiceNotConfigured: Voice not configured. Set up in admin settings.

error.notFoundDescription: This chat session doesn't exist or has been deleted.
error.accessDeniedDescription: You don't have permission to view this chat session.

errorBanner.responseError: There was an error with the response.

onboarding.welcomeTitle: Let's take a moment to get you set up.
onboarding.llmSetupTitle: Almost there! Connect your models to start chatting.
onboarding.completeTitle: You're all set, review the optional settings or click Finish Setup
onboarding.weWillDisplayName: We will display this name in the app.
onboarding.failedToSaveName: Failed to save name. Please try again.
onboarding.onyxSupportsModels: Onyx supports both self-hosted models and popular providers.
onboarding.enableWebSearchDescription: Enable Onyx to search the internet for information.
onboarding.imageGenerationDescription: Set up models to create images in your chats.
onboarding.inviteTeamDescription: Manage users and permissions for your team
